title: Ordered CatBoost on High-Cardinality Race Memory
summary: The dataset has many drivers and race-context combinations, and CatBoost’s ordered boosting plus ordered categorical statistics were designed specifically to reduce prediction shift from target leakage in such settings.
rationale: The dataset has many drivers and race-context combinations, and CatBoost’s ordered boosting plus ordered categorical statistics were designed specifically to reduce prediction shift from target leakage in such settings. This is relevant because manual frequency/cross features may leave performance on the table while naive target encoding would be dangerous.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost in ordered mode with raw categorical columns and a small set of sequence-safe numeric features, emphasizing combos like Driver×Race, Driver×Compound, Race×Compound, and Year×Race. Compare a mostly-raw-cat CatBoost against the current engineered GBDT and blend OOF logits if fold correlation is low enough.
expected_effect: May recover signal from sparse identity-strategy patterns without handcrafting many fragile encodings.
risk: If folds are not race-grouped, ordered target statistics can still look artificially strong; public LB may also punish overfit race identity memory.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
